items.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
items.summary: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.

items.title: Mortgage rates are rising again, but homebuyers are seeing some advantages
items.summary: Mortgage rates last week rose to the highest level since last August, but homebuyer demand climbed as well, as buyers see more supply.

items.title: Sen. Wyden introduces bill to curb Trump's tariff powers
items.summary: Sen. Ron Wyden said Trump has "abused every trade authority at his disposal" and called his new tariffs on Canadian goods a "shakedown."

items.title: Reddit stock sinks on report it may not renew Google AI content deal
items.summary: Google's AI summaries have reduced traffic to websites from the search results page and Reddit is reconsidering the benefits of the deal, the Journal said.

items.title: Samsung ups prices as it launches new foldable smartphones ahead of expected Apple debut
items.summary: Samsung launched the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8.

items.title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic as part of computing power deal
items.summary: Anthropic's agreement with AMD is the latest in a string of infrastructure deals the company has announced this year to increase its computing capacity.

items.title: Peacock is getting a lift from World Cup, Spanish-language content
items.summary: Peacock attracted new subscribers with the Spanish-language telecast of the World Cup, and executives say advertisers are taking notice.

items.title: Trump's generic drug tariffs: What they could mean for medicine prices, manufacturers and supply
items.summary: Trump's generic drug tariff plan raises questions over prices, U.S. manufacturing and which drugmakers stand to gain or lose as details remain unclear.

items.title: $100M New Jersey deli fraudster James Patten sentenced to 21 months in prison
items.summary: The notorious New Jersey deli fraud case led to losses of more than $5 million for victims that included two U.S. universities.

items.title: Renting a yacht in the Mediterranean this summer just got cheaper
items.summary: Summer charters in the Med are down 20% to 30% from last year, yacht brokers say, with a growing number offering special discounts for July and August.

items.title: Samsung details features of its smart glasses as it enters market dominated by Meta
items.summary: The Samsung smart glasses are the tech giant's first foray into the product category which is currently dominated by Meta.

items.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
items.summary: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Wednesday
items.summary: The rise in oil prices cannot be ignored today.

items.title: Citizens United provision allowing foreign campaign contributions draws Democratic bill
items.summary: Rep. Jamie Raskin, D-Md., and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., want to establish foreign ownership thresholds for companies that spend on campaigns.

items.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
items.summary: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

items.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
items.summary: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

items.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
items.summary: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

items.title: Fidelity says 2026 retirees may spend $185,500 on healthcare. One category may push those costs higher
items.summary: A 65-year-old who retires in 2026 may spend an average of $185,500 on health and medical expenses in retirement, according to Fidelity Investments.

items.title: SpaceX snaps 7-day losing streak, sets earnings date that triggers first big share unlock
items.summary: SpaceX's stock gained on Tuesday and looked to snap a seven-day losing streak after announcing Aug. 4 as its maiden earnings report

items.title: OpenAI appoints two new members to board of directors
items.summary: OpenAI appointed longtime financial executives David Vélez and Robin Vince to its nonprofit and for-profit boards.

items.title: Prediction markets and their opponents are spending more to influence Congress in 2026
items.summary: Kalshi and groups representing the casino and gaming industry and online sports betting apps, all have boosted their lobbying spending in 2026.

items.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
items.summary: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

items.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine’s army chief as protests expose wartime power struggle
items.summary: The reshuffle comes after almost a week of nationwide unrest and represents one of the biggest political crises since Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022.

items.title: Goldman Sachs creates private markets platform as rich investors seek the next SpaceX and Stripe
items.summary: Goldman Sachs is launching a new alternative investments platform aimed at giving wealthy clients and family offices direct stakes in private companies.

items.title: India steps up energy shift as costs and tariff pressures mount — oil blending push sparks backlash
items.summary: India is banking on alternative fuels as it prioritizes energy security amid high oil prices and supply constraints.

items.title: Wistron shares surge in Taiwan after opening of Texas AI superchip plant to supply Nvidia
items.summary: Taiwan’s Wistron Corporation opens first U.S. facility in Fort Worth, Texas.

items.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
items.summary: Many server makers are seeing faster growth as companies race to deploy artificial intelligence servers.

items.title: The company behind Post-it notes has found an unlikely role in the AI buildout
items.summary: 3M CEO Bill Brown said its optical connectivity technology can cut fiber installation time by up to 85% as hyperscalers build AI data centers.

items.title: Sony’s 'ironic' PlayStation disc decision upends gamer conventions and threatens a $7 billion resale market
items.summary: In 2013, Sony tweaked Xbox by showing how easy it was to share a PlayStation disc. Now, gamers say Sony has become exactly what it mocked.

items.title: Apple prepares a new way to buy iPhones. Plus, making sense of Google's new AI models
